title: Counting and Natural Numbers
summary: Counting assigns stable ordered quantities to discrete collections.
claim: Discrete quantities can be represented consistently using ordered symbolic quantities called natural numbers.

proof_layers.summary: Counting assigns stable ordered quantities to discrete collections.
proof_layers.intuitive_explanation: Counting follows from one-to-one correspondence and stable successor relationships.
proof_layers.formal_or_structural_proof: Counting follows from one-to-one correspondence and stable successor relationships.
proof_layers.empirical_or_reconstruction_basis: Reconstructable with simple objects, marks, measurement, or geometric construction depending on the truth.
proof_layers.validation_assessment: Level 5 — Mathematical / Logical Proof within arithmetic foundations.
